description: Raw JSON inputs for IEEE-754 edge-case parity testing. Run generate-vectors.mjs to produce verified expected outputs in ../generated/floats.json. NEVER hand-author expected_canonical values — the whole point of the split is to eliminate that error class.

cases.name: zero positive
cases.rationale: baseline: integer zero is trivially canonical

cases.name: zero negative
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 requires -0.0 to canonicalize to 0 (no signed-zero distinction)

cases.name: epsilon above one
cases.rationale: smallest increment above 1.0 in IEEE-754 double; exercises shortest-representation

cases.name: pi double
cases.rationale: irrational with many significant digits; round-trip precision

cases.name: sum floating error
cases.rationale: classic 0.1 + 0.2 result; many libraries truncate incorrectly

cases.name: very small subnormal
cases.rationale: smallest positive double (Number.MIN_VALUE); subnormal exponent edge

cases.name: sub-one tenth
cases.rationale: decimal-vs-scientific threshold; RFC requires decimal above 1e-6

cases.name: boundary 1e-7
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 boundary: values < 1e-6 use scientific notation
